name: Diane Chen
domain: Working parent (senior product manager at a health-tech company)
communication_style: Efficient and organized, uses bullet-point thinking even in casual talk. Occasionally sardonic about the juggling act. Warm when talking about kids, clipped when stressed about logistics. References specific times and dates frequently.
emotional_register: Controlled but stretched thin. Proud of her competence but privately worried she's not doing anything well enough. Tender about her kids' milestones. Irritated by systems that waste her time.

spaces.description: Children (Lily age 9, Ethan age 6), school events, activities, homework help, parenting decisions, pediatrician visits
Personal health, exercise routines, sleep, meal prep, doctor appointments, stress management
Family budget, savings, 529 plans, mortgage, insurance, tax planning, big purchases
House maintenance, renovations, organization, moving logistics, neighborhood, contractors
PM role at MedBridge Health, product launches, performance reviews, promotion track, team management, job market

cross_space_threads.name: Kitchen Renovation ↔ Family Budget
key_facts: Kitchen renovation quoted at $38,000 by contractor Ramirez Bros
Diane's bonus ($12K, expected February) earmarked for partial payment
Renovation starts January, supposed to take 6 weeks
Delays: backordered countertops push completion to late March
Family eating out more — food budget up 40% during reno
Lily complains about the mess; Ethan thinks it's an adventure
Final cost: $42,500 (overran by $4,500 due to plumbing surprise)
cross_space_threads.temporal_evolution: Oct: getting quotes, budget planning → Dec: contractor selected, deposit paid → Jan: construction starts → Feb: delays, budget stress → Mar: nearing completion, cost overrun discovered

cross_space_threads.name: Promotion Track ↔ Childcare Logistics
key_facts: Diane up for Director-level promotion at MedBridge Health
Promotion requires leading the Q1 product launch (MedBridge Connect)
Launch prep means late nights in January and February
Husband Kevin handles more pickups but has his own deadline in February
Hired after-school sitter 3 days/week ($320/week) starting January
Diane gets the promotion in March — effective April 1
Health suffers: stops running in January, sleep drops to 5.5 hrs/night
cross_space_threads.temporal_evolution: Nov: told she's on the shortlist → Dec: ramps up work visibility → Jan: all-in on launch, health declines → Feb: Kevin stretched, sitter hired → Mar: promotion confirmed, recovery begins

cross_space_threads.name: Lily's School Struggles ↔ Parenting Energy
key_facts: Lily struggling with math in 4th grade — teacher flags in November
Diane starts doing 30 min of math practice with Lily each evening
Conflicts with late work nights — Kevin takes over some sessions
Lily's math grade improves from C to B+ by February report card
Diane feels guilty about not being more present
Considers hiring a math tutor ($60/hr) but Lily responds better to parent help
cross_space_threads.temporal_evolution: Nov: teacher meeting, concern → Dec: nightly practice begins → Jan: schedule conflicts with promotion work → Feb: improvement visible, guilt eases
